name: arrays
use_when: Use when the problem primarily involves traversing, updating, or analyzing elements in an array.
template: Iterate through the array, track needed values, and apply the required logic efficiently.
time_complexity: Usually O(n) or O(n log n), depending on sorting or extra operations.

name: strings
use_when: Use when the problem involves text processing, substring analysis, or character-based operations.
template: Traverse the string, compare characters, build substrings, or track frequencies as needed.
time_complexity: Usually O(n) to O(n^2), depending on substring operations.

name: hash_map
use_when: Use when fast lookup, counting, grouping, or tracking previously seen values is needed.
template: Use a dictionary to store counts, indices, or values while traversing the input.

name: two_pointers
use_when: Use when scanning from both ends or maintaining two moving indices in a sorted or structured input.
template: Initialize two pointers and move them based on comparisons or conditions.

name: sliding_window
use_when: Use for contiguous subarray or substring problems, especially when a window expands, shrinks, or stays fixed.
template: Maintain a window with left and right boundaries, update state as it moves, and track the best valid result.

name: binary_search
use_when: Use when the search space is sorted or when an answer can be found by repeatedly halving a monotonic range.
template: Use left, right, and mid pointers to narrow the search space until the answer is found.

name: stack
use_when: Use when the problem requires last-in-first-out behavior, matching pairs, or monotonic processing.
template: Push and pop elements based on conditions while maintaining stack invariants.

name: queue
use_when: Use when the problem requires first-in-first-out processing, level-order traversal, or scheduling.
template: Process elements in arrival order using a queue, often for BFS or ordered simulation.

name: linked_list
use_when: Use when pointer manipulation, node traversal, or in-place structural updates are central.
template: Track current and previous nodes carefully and update links without losing references.

name: tree
use_when: Use when hierarchical data, recursive traversal, or subtree-based reasoning is involved.
template: Use DFS or BFS traversal and compute results from child nodes or levels.

name: graph
use_when: Use when nodes and edges define relationships, connectivity, shortest paths, or traversal problems.
template: Model the structure as a graph and solve using BFS, DFS, topological sort, or shortest-path methods.

name: heap_priority_queue
use_when: Use when repeated access to the minimum or maximum element is needed efficiently.
template: Push candidates into a heap and pop the smallest or largest as required by the problem.
time_complexity: Often O(n log n) or O(k log n).

name: backtracking
use_when: Use when exploring all valid combinations, permutations, or constrained search paths.
template: Build a partial solution recursively, undo choices, and explore all valid branches.
space_complexity: Depends on recursion depth and stored paths.

name: greedy
use_when: Use when making the locally optimal choice at each step leads to a global optimum.
template: Make the best immediate choice, track feasibility, and avoid unnecessary recomputation.
time_complexity: Usually O(n) to O(n log n).

name: dynamic_programming
use_when: Use when the problem has overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure.
template: Define states, transitions, and base cases, then compute results using memoization or tabulation.
space_complexity: Varies by DP table or memoization.

name: recursion
use_when: Use when the problem naturally breaks into smaller instances of the same form.
template: Define a base case and recursive relation, then solve smaller subproblems repeatedly.

name: matrix
use_when: Use when the problem operates on 2D grids, directional traversal, or cell-based state updates.
template: Traverse rows and columns carefully, often using direction vectors or visited tracking.

name: bit_manipulation
use_when: Use when binary representation, masks, toggling bits, or XOR properties simplify the solution.
template: Apply bitwise operations such as AND, OR, XOR, shifts, or masks to transform and inspect values.
